In the superior court Of the state
of Washington, in and for Spokane
county.


Spokane International Railway
Company. a corporation, petitioner,
VS. William III. Head and Mrs. Will
liam II. Head. his wife. John w.
Phillips, an unmarried man, and An
ME Nesmith, an unmarried woman,
defendants.


To William II. Head and Mrs. Will
liam Fl. Head, his wife; John w.
Phillips. an unmarried man, and An
ME Nesmith, an unmarried woman,
defendants:


You, and each of you, will please
take notice that the petitioner, the
Spokane International Railway Com
pany. has filed in the superior court
of the state of Washington. for Spo-
kane county, a petition directed TO the
said court setting forth that the Spo
kane International Railway Company
is corporation organized under the
laws of the state of Washington. en-
gagged in the construction and opera
ton of line of railway beginning in
the city of Spokane, thence easterly
to a point near the town of Rath-
drum, in the county of Kootenai and
state of Idaho, and thence In a north
easterly direction up the valley of
the Mooyie river. TO the international
boundary line between the United
States and Canada; that 1t Is essen
tial for the operation of it's line of
railway. and the accommodation OF
the public using the same, that II ac-
quire those lands situate in Spokane
county. Washington. and described
as lots three 60 and four ca in block
| two CD of River Front Addition to
Spokane Falls (now Spokane). Wash
! ington, for use for its right of way
for the line of It's said railway. and
grounds and terminal yards. That
| the said defendants. William III.
Heald and Mrs. William II. Head. his
wife; John w Phillips. an unmarried
man, and Annie Nesmith. an unmar-
ried woman. appear to be the sole



that said petition prays that the
court impanel a jury to ascertain and
| determine the comensation to be
made in money to tho owner of said
realty. and to all others interested
therein for the taking of said realty
by petitioner for the purposes afore-
said; or. in case a jury be waived as
in other civil cases. In the manner
prescribed by law, then that the com
pensation to be made as aforesaid be
ascertained and determined by the
court or judge thereof.


And you are hereby notified that
on the 10th day of July. 1905, petit
tioner will apply to the superior court
of the state of Washington. for Spo-
kane county. and to the Honorable
William \. Hunckc. judge thereof, at
his courtroom In the city of Spokane,
